desc: remove_young_users deletes users without updating stored positions for subsequent users, so later lookups can target the wrong user or raise IndexError.
add_friend writes to each user record while get_friends reads separate FRIEND_A/FRIEND_B arrays, so friendships added through add_friend are omitted from analysis.
get_unique_ages_sorted returns an unordered list produced from a set despite promising sorted results.
add_user permits duplicate UIDs, making find_user_position ambiguous and causing map-producing functions to silently overwrite records.
Friend APIs accept nonexistent user IDs, allowing dangling relationships and inconsistent user data.
Storing relationship endpoints in separate mutable arrays permits length or positional desynchronization and makes the data model fragile.
All user, index, relationship, and log data is process-global and never reset, making repeated main calls or tests accumulate state and produce incorrect results.
MIXED_LOG stores the same record objects as USERS, so later mutations to nested friend lists or metadata retroactively alter supposed log entries.
mark_inactive encodes status by replacing the user's real age with -1, destroying data and causing age-based operations to misclassify inactive users.
